title: 10 best herbal creams for dizziness

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with dizziness because of its unique blend of natural ingredients that work together to promote balance and reduce symptoms of vertigo. The cream's soothing properties help to calm the nervous system, alleviating feelings of lightheadedness and disorientation. Additionally, the herbal extracts in the cream may help to improve blood flow to the brain, reducing dizziness caused by reduced oxygenation or circulation. By addressing these underlying issues, maidenhair tree creams can provide relief from dizzy spells and promote a sense of stability and calm.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Adiantum extract has antioxidant compounds that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to dizziness and vertigo.
Circulatory effects: The plant contains flavonoids and saponins that may help improve blood circulation and reduce blood pressure, which can alleviate symptoms of dizziness and lightheadedness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum extract has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear or other areas of the body, which can contribute to dizziness and balance disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of saponins, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of dizziness.
Barks: The barks have astringent properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms associated with dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and grind 200g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 50g of sweet almond oil and 30g of beeswax in a heat-proof glass measuring cup.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally to ensure even heating.
Add the ground ginkgo biloba powder to the melted oil mixture and stir well to combine with spatula.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with dizziness because they promote relaxation and calmness in the body. The cream's active ingredients, such as valerenic acid, interact with the brain's chemistry to slow down nervous system activity, which can help alleviate feelings of lightheadedness and disorientation. Additionally, valerian's sedative properties can reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers for dizziness. By soothing the mind and body, herbal valerian creams can help individuals regain their sense of balance and stability, making them an effective natural remedy for managing dizziness.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Valerian root helps to dilate blood vessels, which can improve blood flow to the brain and reduce dizziness caused by inadequate circulation.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in valerian creams may help to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dizziness and vertigo.
Calming and sedative effects: Valerian root has a calming and sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that can exacerbate dizziness and balance issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Valerian plant contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have sedative and anti-anxiety properties, making them effective in soothing dizziness.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Valerian are rich in sesquiterpenes, which are known for their ability to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valerian root extract 10g and combine it with beeswax 20g in a double boiler.
Add coconut oil 50ml and shea butter 30ml to the mixture and heat gently until melted.
Remove from heat and stir in vitamin e oil 5ml and jojoba oil 20ml to enhance moisturizing properties.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and refrigerate for at least 24 hours to allow cooling and hardening.
Once hardened, apply a small amount to the affected area as needed or mix with other creams or lotions for added relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava creams helps with dizziness because of its unique ability to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. The herb kava, native to the Pacific Islands, has been used for centuries to treat a variety of ailments, including anxiety and restlessness. When applied topically in a cream form, kava has been shown to increase feelings of relaxation and tranquility, helping to alleviate symptoms of dizziness such as lightheadedness and vertigo. This calming effect can be particularly helpful for individuals who experience frequent or debilitating episodes of dizziness.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kavalactones, the active compounds found in kava, have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate dizziness and lightheadedness by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in herbal kava creams can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, both of which are thought to contribute to dizziness and vertigo, by neutralizing free radicals and protecting cells from damage.
Muscle relaxants: Kava has been found to have a muscle-relaxing effect, which can be beneficial for individuals who experience dizziness due to muscle tension or spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their sedative properties, kava roots help to calm the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Rhizomes: Rich in flavonoids and lactones, rhizomes contribute to the calming effects of kava, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal creams for dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of dried kava root and combine it with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture at 150°f for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to infuse the kava extract into oil.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding solids and let cool.
Mix 10% of beeswax with remaining coconut oil base in a double boiler setup heating until melted and well combined.
Combine infused kava oil with beeswax mixture stirring until fully incorporated and allowing to solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with dizziness because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to alleviate feelings of lightheadedness and vertigo. The aroma of lavender has a natural sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, common triggers for dizziness. Additionally, the cream's cooling and gentle texture can help to reduce tension in the muscles and joints, further contributing to its ability to alleviate dizziness symptoms. This makes English lavender creams a popular natural remedy for managing dizzy spells.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce anxiety-related dizziness, allowing individuals to feel more grounded and stable.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate symptoms associated with vestibular disorders, such as vertigo, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness.

Sedative and relaxing properties: The sedative effects of lavender can help promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are common triggers for dizziness, allowing individuals to feel more calm and centered.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of essential oils and bioactive compounds that help to calm and soothe the nervous system, providing relief from dizziness.
Seeds: Lavender seeds have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and vertigo, which are often associated with dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers according to their blooming season and dry them for at least seven days or until brittle.
Combine 20 grams of dried lavender flowers with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to allow for infusion and gentle heat generation.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and preserving the infused carrier oil.
Whip 500 grams of beeswax with 300 milliliters of the lavender-infused carrier oil until smooth and creamy, allowing it to cool for at least two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with dizziness because they contain the herb Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower, which has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and relieve anxiety-related symptoms. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, interact with the body's neurotransmitters to promote relaxation and reduce feelings of vertigo and lightheadedness. By soothing the mind and body, maypop creams can help alleviate dizziness and improve overall balance and equilibrium.
remedy_properties: Passionflower: Passionflower helps to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of dizziness and vertigo by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Ginger: Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness and lightheadedness by reducing inflammation in the inner ear and improving blood circulation.
Lavender: Lavender oil helps to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate dizziness caused by anxiety or panic attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calming compounds that help soothe and stabilize the nervous system, reducing symptoms of dizziness.
Barks: Contain astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels and improve circulation, alleviating dizziness caused by poor circulation.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried passionflower herb and carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil in equal parts measuring 20g each.
Grind the dried passionflower herb into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 3 minutes to release its active compounds.
Combine the ground passiflora incarnata with the carrier oils in a small saucepan and heat them over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and let it cool to room temperature.
Whip the cooled cream using an electric mixer until it thickens to desired consistency, taking approximately 2-3 minutes, before transferring it to sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with dizziness because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe the digestive system and calm the nervous system. The oat kernel, in particular, contains a type of fiber called beta-glucan, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. By calming the stomach and intestines, herbal oats creams can help alleviate dizziness caused by digestive issues, such as IBS or acid reflux. Additionally, the cream's gentle, non-irritating texture allows for easy absorption, further reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal oat cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like avenanthramides, which help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate symptoms of dizziness by calming irritated tissues and reducing swelling.

Soothing and calming properties: The moisturizing and emollient effects of herbal oat cream help calm and soothe the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort that can contribute to feelings of dizziness or lightheadedness.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal oat cream contains antioxidants like vitamin E and beta-carotene, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing the likelihood of experiencing dizziness and other symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the oat plant contain saponins, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Barks: The barks of the oat plant contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the symptoms of dizziness caused by inflammation or stress.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried avena sativa oat straw flowers weighing approximately 100 grams.
Combine the dried avena sativa oat straw flowers with 500 milliliters of organic coconut oil in a large glass jar.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 8 weeks, shaking the jar daily and monitoring its color and aroma.
Strain the infused coconut oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining approximately 400 milliliters of liquid.
Mix the strained herbal infusion with 100 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of vitamin e oil, then heat the mixture gently over low heat for approximately 30 minutes to create a smooth, spreadable cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with dizziness because its soothing properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that often contribute to dizziness. The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile also help to reduce blood vessel constriction, which can cause dizziness and lightheadedness. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the digestive system can help alleviate symptoms of vertigo and motion sickness, providing relief from dizzy spells.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and dizziness caused by stress or other factors.
Sedative properties: The sedative effects of chamomile cream can help to soothe and relax the body, which can be especially helpful in alleviating symptoms of vertigo and dizziness.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which may contribute to dizziness and lightheadedness, and also protect against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are used due to their calming and soothing properties, which help alleviate dizziness and promote relaxation.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are used in creams for dizziness because they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that has been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried chamomile flowers in an amount of one ounce per cup of carrier oil.
Infuse the dried chamomille flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba oil for four hours.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the solids from liquids.
Mix equal parts of the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil then heat gently until melted.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for one hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap creams helps with dizziness because they contain Baicalin, a powerful flavonoid that has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system. This natural ingredient helps to reduce feelings of vertigo and lightheadedness by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety levels. Additionally, Skullcap's vasodilatory properties help to improve blood flow to the brain, which can further alleviate symptoms of dizziness. As a result, herbal Skullcap creams offer a natural and effective solution for managing dizziness and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Scutellaria flavonoids: These compounds have been shown to possess antispasmodic and sedative effects, which can help calm nervous system activity and alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Baicalin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear that may be contributing to dizziness and balance issues.
Scutellarein: This flavonoid has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress that may be exacerbating dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the skullcap plant are used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system and help alleviate symptoms of dizziness.

Barks: The barks of the skullcap plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that work synergistically to stabilize blood vessels and improve blood circulation, reducing dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh scutellaria baicalensis roots in the morning after dew has evaporated at a ratio of 10:1.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place for 7 days to reduce moisture content by half.
Grind the dried scutellaria baicalensis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 24 hours of drying.
Mix 30 grams of the powdered scutellaria baicalensis with 150ml of sweet almond oil and 50ml of beeswax in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool to room temperature before whipping into a creamy consistency within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with dizziness because they contain natural ingredients that help to regulate hormones and alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance, a common cause of dizziness. The cream's active compounds, such as cinnamic acid and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing feelings of lightheadedness and vertigo. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to dizziness. By addressing these underlying causes, chaste tree creams provide natural relief from dizziness and improve overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Vasoprotective compounds: Chaste tree cream contains vasoprotective compounds that help to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation, which can reduce symptoms of dizziness caused by poor circulation or orthostatic hypotension.

Corticosteroid-inhibiting properties: The cream's corticosteroid-inhibiting properties may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate stress-related symptoms of dizziness, such as anxiety and nervousness.

Antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds: Chaste tree cream contains antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to relax muscles and reduce inflammation in the neck and head, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness or vertigo.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain a high concentration of iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils, including beta-caryophyllene and terpinen-4-ol, which have been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce dizziness.
Seeds: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Collect dried vitex agnus castus leaves and flowers in amounts of 2 ounces.
Mix the dried herb with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:3 for 30 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 60 minutes to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Add vitamin e oil as a preservative in an amount of 0.1% and store in dark glass containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime creams helps with dizziness because it contains a blend of herbs that work together to calm and soothe the digestive system. Ginger, for example, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and stomach discomfort, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness. Meanwhile, peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the stomach and reduces bloating, further alleviating symptoms of dizziness. The lime flavor also adds a refreshing and calming quality, helping to ease digestive upset and leave you feeling more balanced and grounded.
remedy_properties: Ginger: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with dizziness, making it a natural remedy for alleviating symptoms.
Peppermint: Peppermint's menthol content has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to ease the discomfort and dizziness caused by motion sickness or other gastrointestinal issues.
Lemongrass: Lemongrass contains citral, a compound that has natural antispasmodic properties, which can help to relieve muscle cramps and spasms that may contribute to feelings of dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lime plant contain compounds that help to calm the nervous system, reducing symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.
Barks: The barks of the lime plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate tension and discomfort associated with dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia x europaea flowers at peak bloom in late spring ensuring optimal potency.
Dry the flowers using air circulation and low heat for 7-10 days to preserve their bioactive compounds.
Combine 20g of dried tilia x europaea flowers with 100ml of organic jojoba oil and 50ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 70°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to sterilized containers for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with dizziness because they contain calming compounds that soothe the nervous system. The hop extract in these creams has a sedative effect, which can help to slow down an overactive mind and reduce feelings of anxiety and unease. This, in turn, can alleviate dizziness and vertigo caused by stress or other factors. Additionally, hops creams can also improve blood flow and circulation, which can further contribute to reducing dizziness symptoms. By promoting relaxation and balance, herbal hop creams can provide effective relief from dizziness.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Hops contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and alleviating dizziness caused by hypertension or vasovagal reactions.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in hops cream may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to dizziness and vertigo symptoms.

Sedative and anti-anxiety effects: Hops have been traditionally used as a natural sedative and anti-anxiety agent, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety-related dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to treat digestive issues and nausea, which can be related to dizziness.
Buds: Hops buds contain high levels of beta-caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating vertigo and dizziness.
Seeds: The seeds of the hops plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat anxiety, stress, and insomnia, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried humulus lupulus leaves and 4 ounces of carrier cream in a mortar and pestle.
Grind the humulus lupulus leaves into a fine powder using the mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground humulus lupulus powder with 4 ounces of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches 180°f after 15 minutes of heating.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth into a glass container, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort creams helps with dizziness because of its unique properties that calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of lightheadedness and vertigo. The herb's natural sedative compounds, such as leonurine and beta-sitosterol, work to regulate blood flow and circulation, alleviating dizziness caused by fluctuations in blood pressure or inner ear problems. Additionally, motherwort has anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe the brain and nervous tissues, further reducing symptoms of dizziness and promoting a sense of stability and balance.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Motherwort cream's vasodilatory properties help to dilate blood vessels, which can increase blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, alleviating symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in motherwort cream reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness by improving circulation and reducing blood vessel constriction.

Cardiac toning effects: Motherwort's cardiac toning properties help to strengthen the heart and improve its ability to pump blood efficiently, which can reduce symptoms of dizziness and fainting by regulating blood pressure and circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Motherwort roots are valued for their ability to calm the nervous system, making them a prime choice for treating dizziness-related issues.
Buds: The buds of the motherwort plant contain high levels of volatile oils that can help to soothe and stabilize the body's balance centers, alleviating feelings of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leonurus sibiricus leaves and dry them for at least 30 minutes in shade.
Combine one teaspoon of dried leonurus sibiricus leaves with two tablespoons of olive oil in a small bowl.
Steam-distill the mixture using a double boiler for 30 minutes to extract essential oils.
Mix the extracted oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil, heating gently until the mixture reaches 180°f after five minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and let it cool before use, stored in an airtight container for six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with dizziness because they contain hyperforin, a potent phytochemical that has been shown to have a positive effect on the nervous system. The cream absorbs into the skin and travels to the affected areas of the body, helping to calm and soothe the nerves. This can help alleviate feelings of dizziness, vertigo, and balance issues by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy nerve function. Additionally, St John's wort has natural antispasmodic properties that can help ease muscle spasms in the neck and inner ear, which are common causes of dizziness.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have anticonvulsant and sedative effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce the frequency and severity of dizziness episodes by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid has been found to have vasodilatory effects, which can help to increase blood flow to the brain and improve circulation, potentially reducing symptoms of dizziness caused by poor blood flow.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of hyperforin, a compound that helps to reduce anxiety and stress-related dizziness.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of vertigo.
Flowers: Used for their essential oils, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system and may help to stabilize blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum leaves by cutting stems at a 45-degree angle and collecting fresh material.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or using a dehydrator at 55°c.
Grind dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 20% coconut oil and 80% beeswax, heating the mixture to 60°c for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
Pour the mixture into sterile jars and let it cool at room temperature before sealing for storage and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin creams helps with dizziness because it contains the natural antispasmodic properties of cumin, which help to relax and calm down the nervous system. The cream's soothing and calming effects can ease the symptoms of vertigo and dizziness by reducing muscle tension and promoting healthy circulation. Additionally, cumin has been known to stabilize blood pressure and reduce inflammation, both of which are common causes of dizziness. By applying the cream topically, individuals can quickly and naturally alleviate their symptoms of dizziness and regain a sense of balance and equilibrium.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Cumin's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dizziness by reducing blood flow to the brain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Cumin's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cuminaldehyde, reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of vertigo and dizziness.
Carminative properties: Cumin's essential oils have a carminative effect, which helps relieve gas and bloating that can contribute to feelings of lightheadedness and dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing digestive issues that can contribute to dizziness.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and stabilize blood vessels, alleviating symptoms of lightheadedness.
Seeds: Packed with omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants, supporting overall cardiovascular health and reducing the risk of dizziness caused by poor circulation.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of cuminum cyminum seeds into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil in a glass bowl until well combined.
Add 2 drops of lavender essential oil and 2 drops of peppermint essential oil to the mixture and stir gently.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes or until warm and smooth.
Pour the cream into a sterilized glass jar and store it in refrigerator for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with dizziness because of its natural ability to stimulate blood flow and circulation. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to dilate blood vessels, allowing for increased oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the brain. This can help alleviate feelings of lightheadedness and dizziness by improving overall circulation and reducing symptoms of poor blood flow. Additionally, ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that may also contribute to its ability to relieve dizziness.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic Properties: Ginger's antiemetic compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to calm the stomach and alleviate nausea and dizziness by reducing the production of chemicals that stimulate vomiting.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the inner ear and body, which can contribute to dizziness and balance disorders, helping to promote a sense of stability and equilibrium.

Vasodilatory Properties: Ginger's vasodilatory compounds help to dilate blood vessels and improve blood circulation, which can increase oxygenation of the brain and nervous system, reducing symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, also known as the underground stem, is a good source of compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Roots: Ginger roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can help to calm the stomach and reduce feelings of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Grate 2 inches of fresh ginger root and mix it with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Steam-distill the mixture for 20 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Combine the extracted liquid with 8 ounces of beeswax and 6 ounces of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax melts and blends smoothly.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and let it cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with dizziness because of its calming and soothing properties. The gentle, non-greasy texture absorbs quickly into the skin, providing instant relief from feelings of lightheadedness and vertigo. The herbal extract is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, further alleviating symptoms of dizziness. Additionally, the cream's natural ingredients have a positive impact on blood flow and circulation, which can help to stabilize and ground the body, reducing feelings of wooziness and disorientation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Damask rose creams contain antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to dizziness by improving blood flow and reducing blood vessel constriction.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate symptoms of vertigo and dizziness by reducing inflammation in the inner ear or brain, which can cause balance problems.

Calming and sedative effects: Damask rose extracts have a calming and sedative effect, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that may exacerbate dizziness, promoting relaxation and improved overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils, roots are often used to create sedative and calming properties in creams that help alleviate dizziness.
Fruits: The fruit's pulp contains vitamins and minerals that help regulate blood pressure and circulation, making it effective for treating dizziness-related conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena petals at dawn for their highest essential oil content.
Dry the petals in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours to preserve quality.
Combine 250g of dried petals with 100ml of carrier oil like sweet almond or coconut and heat gently.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes under low heat then filter and discard solids.
Mix equal parts of the infused oil with beeswax and a few drops of lavender essential oil to create a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with dizziness because of its ability to promote blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the body. The natural oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have potent antioxidant properties that help to calm the nervous system and alleviate feelings of lightheadedness. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory properties can help to ease vertigo and balance issues by reducing swelling and discomfort in the inner ear, ultimately promoting a sense of stability and equilibrium.
remedy_properties: Antihistaminic property: Thyme's antihistaminic property helps to reduce histamine levels in the body, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness by calming down blood vessels and improving blood circulation.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of thyme help to relax muscle spasms and cramps that can cause dizziness, including those in the neck and shoulder muscles that can lead to vertigo.

Vasoconstrictive property: Thyme's vasoconstrictive effect helps to constrict blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate feelings of lightheadedness and dizziness by increasing oxygenation to the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been traditionally used in herbal remedies for their sedative properties, making them effective in calming symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.
Seeds: Thyme seeds contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can alleviate the symptoms of dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves measure one ounce of dried herb or two ounces of fresh herb.
Combine the harvested thymus vulgaris with one-fourth cup of distilled water and let it steep for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Mix the resulting liquid with three tablespoons of beeswax pellets and two tablespoons of coconut oil heating gently until melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers filling to the top and letting it cool and solidify completely taking approximately 30-45 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with dizziness because its natural essential oils have a calming effect on the nervous system. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling in the inner ear, which is often a contributing factor to dizziness and vertigo. Additionally, marjoram's soothing properties can ease tension and anxiety, which are common triggers for dizziness episodes. By applying the cream topically, users can experience quick relief from these uncomfortable symptoms and regain their balance and sense of stability.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Wild marjoram creams help relieve muscle spasms and cramps that can contribute to feelings of dizziness, reducing their frequency and severity.
Vasoconstrictive properties: The cream's ability to constrict blood vessels helps improve circulation and reduce the sensation of lightheadedness or faintness associated with dizziness.
Antioxidant properties: Wild marjoram's antioxidant compounds help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dizziness and other symptoms of vertigo.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to calm and soothe the nervous system, making them effective in treating symptoms of dizziness.
Barks: The barks contain anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried origanum vulgare leaves and flowers for optimal potency and flavor.
Combine one part origanum vulgare herb with three parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the herbal mixture for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, until the oil turns a deep greenish-yellow color.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with dizziness because of its natural calming properties, which can help to reduce feelings of nausea and vertigo. The herb's soothing and relaxing effects can ease anxiety and stress, common triggers for dizziness, allowing the body to regulate its balance more effectively. Additionally, lemon balm's mild sedative qualities can help to slow down rapid heartbeat and improve blood flow, further alleviating symptoms of dizziness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties can help to relax muscles and calm nervous spasms, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can exacerbate dizziness and vertigo symptoms.

GABA-enhancing properties: Lemon balm contains compounds that enhance GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) activity, a neurotransmitter that helps regulate muscle relaxation and calmness, which can help to alleviate dizziness and anxiety-related episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, roots are used to create a grounding and balancing effect, helping to alleviate dizziness.
Leaves: Known for their calming properties, leaves are used to soothe digestive issues that can contribute to dizziness, promoting overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves from your garden or a trusted supplier in the morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the fresh melissa officinalis leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or air-drying them for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt 200g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat while stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
Mix the ground melissa officinalis powder with the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture, stirring until well combined and smooth.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dizziness are topical remedies that utilize natural ingredients to alleviate symptoms of vertigo, lightheadedness, and balance issues. These creams work by promoting blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and calming the nervous system, which helps to stabilize the body's equilibrium. For example, peppermint oil and ginger cream have been shown to reduce dizziness in patients with vertigo. By using these creams, individuals can regain their sense of balance and confidence, improving their daily lives and allowing them to participate in activities they previously found challenging or avoided due to their dizziness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dizziness with herbal creams is to focus on calming and balancing the body's natural energies. Ginseng and licorice root creams can help alleviate symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting circulation. Peppermint and ginger creams can also provide relief by soothing digestive issues, a common cause of dizziness. Always choose high-quality, pure herbal products and follow the manufacturer's instructions for use.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dizziness that are treated with herbal creams are nausea, vertigo, and lightheadedness. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as ginger, peppermint, and passionflower help alleviate these symptoms by calming the digestive system, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. Additionally, creams infused with essential oils like lavender and chamomile can also provide a sense of calm and relief from dizziness-like symptoms.
